red_lines: Subordination of Japan to Western powers
core_narratives: Unbroken Imperial line from the Sun Goddess Amaterasu
Japan's divine destiny to lead Asia
Emperor as spiritual father of the Japanese nation

decision_history.event_description: Imperial Conference debated war with the United States and Western powers
decision_history.decision_taken: Gave imperial sanction to war against United States, Britain, and Netherlands after military presented operational plans
decision_history.outcome: Pearl Harbor attack launched; initial six months of stunning Japanese victories
context_factors: military presented war as only viable option
institutional pressure from Army and Navy

decision_history.event_description: Japanese forces advancing in Philippines and Southeast Asia
decision_history.decision_taken: Personally pressed commanders for faster operations against Bataan and authorized expanded operations
decision_history.outcome: Fall of Bataan and Corregidor; Japanese control of Western Pacific consolidated
context_factors: divine authority lending weight to military demands

decision_history.event_description: Mounting losses after Midway and Guadalcanal suggested war momentum shifting
decision_history.decision_taken: Authorized continued offensive operations and approved Three Alls policy in China despite deteriorating position
decision_history.outcome: Continued attrition of irreplaceable naval assets; brutal occupation policies in China
context_factors: no mechanism for strategic retreat in Japanese military culture

information_sources: Lord Keeper of the Privy Seal
cultural_context.domestic_power_base: Divine imperial institution, Shinto religious establishment, military command structure, palace bureaucracy (Imperial Household Ministry)

military_posture.force_disposition: overextended across vast Pacific perimeter with diminishing naval superiority
